opportunity_summary: Google's 3rd Nebraska data center. 2M sq ft on 570+ acres. Could require 1-3GW power (more than all of Lincoln). $1.2B+ investment. Cooling design not yet public — Google has deployed 1GW of liquid cooling globally for TPU pods. Pre-decision opportunity for cooling vendors.
confidence_reason: Google confirmed involvement (DCD), Flatwater Free Press, Journal Star report scale. Cooling system not disclosed for this specific site. Google's global liquid cooling deployment is confirmed separately.
source_1_title: Google confirms involvement in 2 million sq ft data center project in Lincoln, Nebraska
source_1_evidence_snippet: Google confirmed involvement in 2 million sq ft data center project. 570+ acres in Lancaster County.
source_2_title: Google proposes Nebraska data center requiring more power than all of Lincoln
source_2_evidence_snippet: Could require 1,000 to 3,000 megawatts of power, more than 800 MW needed across Lincoln Electric System.
notes: Cooling design not yet public for this site. Google has 1GW liquid cooling deployed globally for TPU pods and contributing cooling tech to OCP (Project Deschutes CDU). Scale suggests liquid cooling will be required for AI workloads. Originally filed as Agate LLC in 2019.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Could require 1,000 to 3,000 megawatts of power.

facility_name: Alcove Osawatomie Data Center — Miami County, KS (Predevelopment)
full_address: 115 acres, north of Osawatomie, Miami County, KS
opportunity_summary: Early-stage predevelopment. 115 acres, 600K sq ft, 50-150MW, $900M-$1.1B. No construction approved, no land sold. 3-year exclusive development agreement. 1M gallons water/day + 150MW power required. Kansas SB 98 tax exemption (20yr) in effect. Very early opportunity window.
confidence_reason: DCD and local news (KSHB) report predevelopment agreement. No construction, no final agreement, no confirmed tenant. City requires 1M gallons/day water and 150MW power — both unconfirmed as available.
source_1_title: 115-acre data center could come to Osawatomie, Kansas, says local government
source_1_evidence_snippet: Alcove Development predevelopment agreement. 115 acres, 600,000 sq ft, 50-150MW capacity, $900M-$1.1B investment.
source_2_title: Rural Miami County, Kansas, residents respond to $1 billion data center proposal
source_2_evidence_snippet: City requires 1 million gallons of water and 150 megawatts of power per day. 5 MW renewable energy credits via adjacent solar array.
notes: Very early stage — no construction, no land sale, no tenant. Kansas SB 98 (July 2025) provides 20-year tax exemption. Miami County Commission considered construction moratorium. Cooling tech undecided = opportunity window but high risk of project not proceeding.
pipeline_evidence_snippet: Predevelopment agreement. No land sold, no construction approved, no final agreement executed.
